# Decdock

Decdock is a corporate memory engine for companies. Its first wedge is a source-linked decision registry: it turns scattered emails and meeting notes into auditable records of what was decided, who ratified it, when, why, and how later decisions changed it. The same engine direction also supports reusable institutional knowledge: extracted facts, source trails, freshness signals, conflicts, and human review.

## Key facts

- Decision registry: extracts candidate decisions, verifies ratification in a second pass, and keeps unratified suggestions out of the official registry.
- Knowledge memory direction: decisions and reusable business knowledge are treated as related memory objects with provenance, dates, conflicts, supersession, and review state.
- Source-linked by design: every accepted record is tied to the source sentence or source pointer; uncertain items remain transparent review candidates.
- Decision graph: policies, decisions, conflicts, supersession, compliance, and violations can be explored as a connected graph.
- Live static demos: public graph and demo pages show the interaction model with representative data only; they do not call live LLMs or APIs.
- Sources today: email threads and meeting notes. English landing and proof pages are live; Turkish content remains available.
- Entry offer: a limited-scope pilot or one-time Decision Audit from a small project slice, without broad integration or system access.
- ROI stance: Decdock does not invent universal savings numbers. It shows where decision loss, stale knowledge, ownerless decisions, and repeated work create measurable risk so the customer can calculate impact from their own examples.
- Security/deployment posture: starts with limited-scope report mode; later deployment can be managed cloud or customer-owned cloud/server depending on sensitivity and procurement.
- Access model: executives receive risk/digest views; teams search records they are allowed to see; admins control sources, retention, users, and visibility by role/scope.
- NOT: a meeting-notes tool, task manager, surveillance system, or generic enterprise search assistant. Summary tools answer "what was discussed?" Decdock preserves "what was decided or learned, where it came from, and whether it is still valid."

## Decdock vs summary tools

- Summary and assistant tools produce ephemeral summaries of conversations. Decdock creates durable, source-linked memory records that can be reviewed, challenged, superseded, and audited.
- They are complementary: assistants help with productivity; Decdock handles accountability and institutional memory.
- Roadmap direction: when an AI assistant connects to Decdock, it should query verified memory records rather than guess from raw conversation history.

## Stage

Early-stage, pilot-ready.

## Contact

pilot@decdock.com

## Language

The main landing page is English by default for international visitors. The Turkish landing page remains available at https://decdock.com/tr/.
